rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE KING et al VS. AC AND S,INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For An Order Requiring Pltf To File An Undertaking To Secure An Aware For Costs Pursuant To Ccp Section 1030
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 2, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT VOLVO CARS OF NORTH AMERICA, L.L.C. AND VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION For An Order Requiring PLAINTIFF To File An Undertaking To Secure An Aware For Costs Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 1030 IS DENIED. BY NOT ATTACHING THE RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S INTERROGATORIES, THE COURT IS UNABLE TO DETERMINE IF DEFENDANT IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL. FURTHERMORE, C.C.P. SECTION 1030 IS FOR THE BENEFIT OF CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN THAT IT IS A RESIDENT OF CALIFORNIA. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF HAS DEMONSTRATED THE FINANCIAL INABLILITY TO SECURE A BOND. UNDER C.C.P SECTION 995.240, THE COURT WOULD EXCUSE THE POSTING OF A BOND IF THE REQUISITE SHOWING HAD BEEN MADE. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN PATRICK MONETTE-SHAW VS. SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF SUPERVISORS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner'S Petition For Alternative Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, APRIL 2, 2008 LINE 20. PETITIONER'S PETITION FOR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDAMUS: HEARING REQUIRED. THE PARTIES SHOULD DISCUSS (1) THE APPLICABILITY OF MONETTE-SHAW V S.F. BOARD OF SUPERVISORS (2006) 139 CAL APP 4TH 1210 T0 THE FIRST, SECOND AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION. (2) PETITIONER'S STANDING TO CHALLENGE THE CHAMBERS SETTLEMENT. (3) THE STEPS TAKEN BY RESPONDENT TO ADDRESS PETITIONER'S PROCEDURAL OBJECTIONS. (4) THE BASIS FOR A PRIVATE RIGHT OF ACTION FOR THE THIRD THROUGH SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. =(302/PJM/VC)
